title: Pixir Presenter Workspace Set v1
description: Two-workspace Workspace Overview surfaces: bootstrap shell embed, scoped snapshot envelopes (list and detail), scoped error envelope, and set-mode SSE invalidation frame. Each envelope embeds the corresponding single-mode document verbatim: the pixir.monitor.runs v1 list document on the list route, the pixir.presenter.run.v1 document on the detail route. Frozen on #339; consumers #349 and #340.

workspace_key.description: Operator-assigned source key, declared at serve time as --workspace <key>=<path>. Exact UnitIdentity safe-component rule; ASCII-only, so the code-point bound equals the 256-byte bound. Doubles as the operator-facing label; no separate display-name in v1.

shell_config.description: Embedded by the set-mode bootstrap shell (GET /) as HTML-attribute-escaped JSON in a data-workspace-set attribute on the shell's <main> element — no new inline script; the hashed bootstrap script and CSP are unchanged. The SPA reads and strictly validates it once at boot: present and valid = set mode; absent = single mode; present but malformed = explicit boot error, never a silent fallback. Single-workspace shells embed nothing and stay byte-identical to today. Keys are listed in declaration order, which is the stable section order everywhere.

source_facts.description: New per-source facts introduced by the set. sessions_directory is the provenance fact for zero (#365 decision 6): 'observed' means the .pixir/sessions directory exists and counts are real observations (including a real 0); 'absent' means no sessions directory was observed, making a mistyped root diagnosable instead of a silent zero. Carries no judgment and is never an attention condition. The envelope adds NO timestamp: the per-source observed-at is client-held receipt state, outside this schema.

runs_list_document.description: The pixir.monitor.runs v1 list document exactly as single-workspace GET /api/runs produces it. It has no standalone JSON Schema today; this def pins the top-level discriminators, the required inventory quartet, and the projection-metadata keys production always enriches (projected_runs, non_parent_logs, dropped_logs — optional here because bare-rows providers synthesize only the quartet). Row shape stays owned by the list producer; the inventory metadata is the wire home of the per-source confessions and passes through verbatim.

scoped_list_snapshot.description: 200 body of GET /api/workspaces/:key/runs. snapshot embeds the pixir.monitor.runs v1 list document verbatim — structurally identical to the single-mode response after normalizing the volatile clock stamps (projected_at/observed_at) via the projector's test seams, per the fixture sanitization contract (ws-passthrough-parity). The envelope adds scope and never re-derives.

scoped_run_snapshot.description: 200 body of GET /api/workspaces/:key/runs/:id. snapshot embeds the pixir.presenter.run.v1 document verbatim, pinned by $ref to the untouched run.v1 schema; parity with single mode is defined modulo the same clock-stamp normalization.

scoped_error.description: Non-200 JSON body on set-mode API routes. Per-source failure isolation: a failed source answers 503 on its own scoped routes only; there is no set-level 503. Closed kind enum with per-kind details requirements (encoded below): workspace_unavailable (503) requires details.workspace, optional sanitized details.reason; run_not_found (404) requires details.workspace and details.run_id; workspace_not_found (404) requires details.workspace (the charset-validated requested key); invalid_run_id (400) requires details.workspace, allows details.max_bytes; invalid_workspace_key (400) and unscoped_route_unavailable (404) carry no details (a charset-invalid key is never echoed). details.workspace is always the declared label — never a basename or path fragment. Single-workspace mode keeps its post-#371 workspace_basename shape, untouched, as a separate contract case.

invalidation_frame.description: data payload of the set-mode SSE frame on the ONE shared /api/events stream. Strict frame-shape change: the client validates by exact key set projection_id,type,workspace (mirroring today's projection_id,type). The single-mode frame {type, projection_id} is untouched and is a separate contract case; crossed negative fixtures pin both directions. A valid frame invalidates the named source only; any anomaly (malformed frame, unknown workspace key, duplicate sequence id, reorder, gap) forces an authoritative refetch of all declared sources and never creates a source section.
